texto: So what did you do when death came to your house? We continued in the same way as before. What is that, a failure of the imagination? Are you in denial? This is not wholly true; we continue in the same way as before but in parenthesis. My thinking has switched its grammar. The present continuous is its single operational tense. Uncertainty is our present and our future.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Marion Coutts que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “So what did you do when death came to your house? We continued in the same way as before. What is…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, cancer, death, com foco em luto e death.

texto: Childbirth is nothing. Death is mighty. The 0 to our 1. There is nothing in the world like it. But there is nothing in it that is not like the world.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Marion Coutts: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Childbirth is nothing. Death is mighty. The 0 to our 1. There is nothing in the world like it. Bu…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, death-and-dying, nothing pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e reflexao.

texto: What is coping? This is what it is like: a cave underground deep in rock, hung across its roof with accretions of dripping salts. I am cavernous and hard as mineral. The cave holds a pool of dark water that has not seen light. The water is very cold; it is undrinkable and its size is unmapped. It is mine, but people cannot see it. Only Ev sometimes senses that it is there. All the time people say that I am coping very well. It is impossible to explain my strategy to them. It is opaque even to me.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Marion Coutts: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “What is coping? This is what it is like: a cave underground deep in rock, hung across its roof wi…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, coping, loss pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e reflexao-noturna.

frase_original: What is coping? This is what it is like: a cave underground deep in rock, hung across its roof with accretions of dripping salts. I am cavernous and hard as mineral. The cave holds a pool of dark water that has not seen light. The water is very cold; it is undrinkable and its size is unmapped. It is mine, but people cannot see it. Only Ev sometimes senses that it is there. All the time people say that I am coping very well. It is impossible to explain my strategy to them. It is opaque even to me.

texto: Maintaining the thinnest facade of a functioning family that tries to act as others do - plan ahead, drive somewhere, go on holiday, relax - is beyond us. We are smashed. Insecurity jams the gears on every action. Each time we are toppled. I feel a fool over and over again for trying.
explicacao: Citação de Marion Coutts sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Maintaining the thinnest facade of a functioning family that tries to act as others do - plan ahe…” articula familia, cancer, illness em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como illness.
